Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial; Advancing The Current Trial Date; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-19-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Jack Crisp
Allison E. Mullings of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-362-2580) for defendant / opposing party American Honda Motor Co., Inc.
Anthony J. Calero of LOMBARDI, LOPER & CONANT (510-433-2600) for defendant Mendo Mills & Lumber Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL; ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No objection made. The Court adopted its tentative ruling and made it part of the preference order: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a), (d). The November 17, 2014 trial date is advanced to September 23, 2014 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last Day (120 days) is October 17, 2014. Last day for hearing motions for summary judgment/adjudication is September 18, 2014. Plaintiffs have stipulated to shortened notice for such motions. Motions for summary judgment/adjudication may be brought on 15 days notice and hearings set on 15 days before trial, oppositions due 7 calendar days before the hearing, replies due 3 calendar days before the hearing. For any written discovery, the time to respond is shortened to 20 days. E-service is considered the equivalent of personal service. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Further, before any hearing regarding a discovery issue, the parties shall have an informal conference with the Court. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than July 18, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 25, 2014. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on September 18, 2014. Fact discovery cut-off date is Sept. 8, 2014. Expert discovery cut-off date is Sept. 19, 2014. No later than July 1, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than July 1, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference set on July 23, 2014 at 2:00p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. Defendants need to inform plaintiff if there is any discovery dispute. If the C.M.C. is not needed, plaintiff has the responsibility to notify the Court to take it off calendar no later than 2 p.m. the day before the conference. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on Sept. 23, 2014 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by Sept. 18, 2014 by 2:00p.m. For trial preparation all parties must comply with the 5/7/14 Trial Management Order. Mrs. Crisp's deposition will start no later than Aug. 8. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Leticia A. Ralls of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #10070 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
